business.headline: Before Sebi stepped in, Rajesh Exports had already raised eyebrows for yearsSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports has brought fresh scrutiny to its reported revenues and subsidiary disclosures. The action has revived years of public questions over how the company matched vast turnover with thin profits.

The hidden inflation nobody is talking about: Transport inflationAs transportation costs increase across the supply chain, businesses eventually pass those higher expenses on to consumers. The result is 'invisible inflation' – a force that pushes up the cost of almost everything but is rarely talked about on its own.

RBI MPC meeting: 7 key takeaways on repo rate, inflation, growth and moreThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced its latest monetary policy decisions, keeping the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and maintaining a neutral stance. Here are seven key takeaways from the RBI's June MPC meeting.

Rajesh Exports shares hit 5% lower circuit for 2nd straight day after Sebi orderRajesh Exports shares hit the 5% lower circuit for a second straight session after Sebi's interim order. The sell-off reflects investor caution as the regulator examines alleged revenue inflation and orders a fresh forensic audit.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: What it means for your home loan and EMIFor millions of home loan borrowers, the RBI's repo rate decision is closely watched because it can influence borrowing costs. With the central bank keeping the repo rate steady at 5.25%, should you expect any change in your monthly EMI?

RBI rolls out multiple measures to attract more dollars amid rupee weaknessThe RBI has unveiled a package of bond, swap and deposit measures to draw more dollars into India while keeping the repo rate unchanged. The steps are aimed at easing foreign currency liquidity strains as oil prices rise and investor outflows weigh on the rupee.

Higher crude prices forcing a rethink on inflation and growth outlook, says RBIThe RBI kept the repo rate unchanged as Sanjay Malhotra warned that the West Asia conflict is lifting oil prices and disrupting supplies. The central bank has cut its growth forecast and raised its inflation outlook as cost pressures begin to build.

Centre exempts foreign investors from capital gains tax on govt bondsThe tax break is aimed at boosting foreign investment in government bonds, improving post-tax returns for overseas investors and helping attract more stable capital flows into India.

RBI keeps repo rate steady at 5.25%, reduces GDP growth projection for FY27All six members of the MPC voted unanimously to maintain the policy repo rate at 5.25%. The central bank also retained its "neutral" policy stance, signalling that future decisions will depend on incoming economic data and evolving global developments.

PwC review flags accounting irregularities in IndusInd Bank treasury operationsA confidential PwC review reportedly found that manual treasury entries at IndusInd Bank offset trading losses and created a Rs 2,201.76 crore receivable pool. The findings have raised fresh questions over hedging links, accounting treatment and controls around manual adjustments.

Markets open higher as investors await RBI rate decision; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 191.96 points, or 0.26%, to 74,551.97 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 39 points, or 0.17%, to 23,455.55.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall or rise on RBI MPC decision?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,565.00 at 8:17 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 could open above its previous close of 23,416.55.

Gujarat wire-free cities plan: Urban power lines to go underground by 2030Gujarat has approved a statewide mission to move urban power lines underground by 2030. The phased project aims to improve power reliability and safety while making cities less cluttered.

RBI ends FEMA probe against Myntra. Here's what happenedAccording to the Enforcement Directorate (ED), the RBI passed the order on April 20, 2026 under Section 15 of FEMA after receiving a "No Objection" from the agency. As a result, the investigation against the company has been terminated.

LIC owns 10% of Rajesh Exports, shares fall 1% at closing bell amid Sebi allegationsShareholding data for the March 2026 quarter shows that Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC), the country's largest insurer and institutional investor, holds a 10.80% stake in Rajesh Exports.

Revenues are correct, says Rajesh Exports amid Sebi allegationsThe company's response after Sebi issued an interim order against Rajesh Exports and its promoter Rajesh Mehta, raising concerns about the company's financial reporting and questioning whether a large part of its reported revenues had adequate supporting documents.

Sensex, Nifty end flat ahead of RBI policy decision; Titan jumps 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 13.84 points, or 0.02%, to close at 74,360.01, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 10.95 points, or 0.05%, to settle at 23,416.55.

A gold empire, a Swiss refinery and SEBI's Rs 15.15 lakh crore allegationSebi has questioned Rajesh Exports over Rs 15.15 lakh crore of subsidiary revenue between FY21 and FY25. The interim order has intensified scrutiny of the Valcambi owner and sent its shares to the lower circuit.

India plans bond tax break to attract foreign moneyForeign investors have withdrawn nearly $28 billion from Indian equities this year. While foreign money has continued to flow into government bonds, with net investments of around $1.4 billion, policymakers appear keen to attract more stable capital into the debt market.

Why the RBI may avoid a rate cut this time: 3 things to knowAs the Reserve Bank of India prepares to announce its latest monetary policy decision, expectations of another rate cut appear to be fading. Most experts now believe the central bank will keep the repo rate unchanged and continue with a wait-and-watch approach. Here are three reasons why.

entertainment.headline: Why did Shilpa Shinde file false sexual harassment case? Revisiting the Bhabiji row On a recent podcast, Shilpa Shinde said the sexual harassment case she filed against producer Sanjay Kohli was false and stemmed from her dispute with the Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain! makers. Her remarks have revived the long-running controversy.

Friend's death pushed Deepika to do more for mental health: Father Prakash Padukone Former Badminton player Prakash Padukone said his actor, daughter Deepika started The Live Love Laugh Foundation after a friend's death. He said her family supported her going public on depression to widen mental health awareness.

Ashoke Pandit reacts to Shilpa Shinde row: False allegations harm genuine victims Shilpa Shinde's admission that her complaint against Sanjay Kohli was false has drawn a sharp response from Ashoke Pandit. The fallout has reignited scrutiny of the 2016 dispute and the damage caused by false allegations.

Exclusive: Nicholas Galitzine promises to shoot He-Man sequel in India In an exclusive conversation with India Today, Masters of the Universe stars Nicholas Galitzine and Camila Mendes spoke about the film's emotional core, dealing with self-doubt, and why India could be the perfect destination for a future sequel.

Esha Deol praises Bobby Deol after watching Bandar: You're in the best form ever Esha Deol praised Bobby Deol's performance in Bandar after attending a special Mumbai screening. The film hits cinemas today.

VIDEO: He-Man Nicholas Galitzine on shooting sequel in India, Bollywood |Camila Mendes|Masters of the Universe In an exclusive chat with Bhavna Agarwal, Nicholas Galitzine and Camila Mendes open up about becoming He-Man and Teela, overcoming self-doubt, redefining masculinity, and why a future sequel should be shot in India with only one condition: a Bollywood dance number.

Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai review: David Dhawan's formula finally expiresRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 David Dhawan returns to the formula that made him Bollywood's king of comedy - mistaken identities, chaotic romances and jokes that exist in their own universe. The problem is that the audience no longer does. What once felt outrageously entertaining now feels like a relic trying desperately to pass off as nostalgia.

Taylor Swift drops I Knew You song with a rare childhood video. Watch Taylor Swift released I Knew It, I Knew You from Toy Story 5 and shared a rare childhood video. The post underscored her bond with the franchise and the character Jessie's emotional pull before the film's release on June 19.

Brown review: Karisma Kapoor's comeback is dark, messy and magneticRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Brown series review: Karisma Kapoor returns in a psychological crime drama that looks beyond the murders. More than a mystery, Brown is a story of loss told through the eyes of a broken cop.

Hina Khan's sarcastic note to PM after Shilpa Shinde's cancer jibe Hina Khan has escalated her ongoing feud with Shilpa Shinde with a pointed post addressed to India's top leadership. The message follows Shilpa's veiled remarks about Hina's cancer diagnosis and family tragedy in a recent Instagram video.

The Pyramid Scheme review: A funny, frightening look at Indians' easy-money dreamsRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 TVF's The Pyramid Scheme perfectly captures the struggles of everyday Indians who get lured into MLM traps for quick money. It is funny, deeply relatable, and exposes the ugly truth behind financial scams.

Bandar review: Tough questions and easy answers in Bobby Deol's unsettling filmRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Bandar movie review: Anurag Kashyap's Bandar follows fading star Samar Mehra (Bobby Deol) as a rape accusation pushes him into police custody and jail. The film dwells on systemic humiliation and prison brutality, even as its moral perspective narrows.

Aamir Khan gave up Bollywood romance 18 years ago. He never gave up on love though Aamir Khan is set to marry partner Gauri Spratt on July 5. The development underlines the contrast between his off-screen life and his long move away from on-screen romance.

Masters of the Universe review: Nicholas Galitzine's He-man is more than muscleRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Masters of the Universe brings He-Man back to theatres as Prince Adam confronts Skeletor and his own doubts. The film reframes the franchise's idea of power through vulnerability, restraint and responsibility.

Amid RJ Balaji's Karuppu row, actor Suriya shares cryptic note on kindness Suriya shared a cryptic Instagram note on love and kindness as Karuppu makers faced criticism over their remarks about Vijay. The post added to a widening debate over credit for the film even as Karuppu continued its strong box-office run.

The Peddi question: Janhvi Kapoor has the projects, where are the performances? Janhvi Kapoor's back-to-back Telugu films with Jr NTR and Ram Charan have renewed questions about her performances and the roles she is taking. The scrutiny deepens in Peddi, where objectification and consent issues overshadow her character.

Parthiban meets CM Vijay months after his dig at Trisha's wedding outing Filmmaker-actor R Parthiban met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay and gifted him a handwritten letter and a custom painting following his election victory. The meeting comes months after Parthiban's remarks about Trisha Krishnan triggered controversy and drew criticism online.

Gullak Season 5 review: New paint on walls, the same warmth withinRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Gullak Season 5 returns to Mishra Niwas as renovation, family shifts and new visitors reshape familiar routines. The season stays gentle and reflective, finding meaning in change, discomfort and old memories.

Peddi box office Day 1: Ram Charan's film tops Dhurandhar, Pathaan on opening day Peddi box office Day 1: Peddi has given Ram Charan the box-office boost he was looking for. The sports action drama raced past Rs 135 crore worldwide on its opening day, surpassing the likes of Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar and Shah Rukh Khan's Pathaan.

general news.headline: Will start a movement against cult politics: Annamalai after BJP exit Live UpdatesK Annamalai, former Tamil Nadu BJP president, ended speculation about his political future on Friday as he resigned from the party. In his resignation letter, Annamalai confirmed what was already known - disagreement with the leadership over BJP's strategy in Tamil Nadu.

'Adviser' Mamata at centre of cracks in rebel Trinamool campCracks have appeared in the Ritabrata Banerjee-led TMC rebel camp over Mamata Banerjee's role. The dispute has exposed tensions within the bloc as several MLAs insist Mamata remain the supreme leader and not as an 'adviser' as suggested by Ritabrata.

Before Sebi stepped in, Rajesh Exports had already raised eyebrows for yearsSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports has brought fresh scrutiny to its reported revenues and subsidiary disclosures. The action has revived years of public questions over how the company matched vast turnover with thin profits.

Pause AI development: Anthropic says Claude is improving so fast it may not need humansAnthropic says its Claude AI is no longer just helping humans write code. It is increasingly helping build the next generation of AI systems. The company believes this could dramatically speed up AI development while making human oversight and safety safeguards more important than ever.

How Sunil Dutt saved the Bandra slum suburbia, built a Congress vote bankAs Mumbai demolishes slums from Garib Nagar to Goregaon, an old debate has returned. Did Sunil Dutt protect the poor, or help institutionalise Mumbai's slum politics? Here's a look at how Dutt's Partition trauma, Congress's patronage and suburban migration reshaped Bandra, and Mumbai's politics itself.

Bandar review: Tough questions and easy answers in Bobby Deol's unsettling filmRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026Bandar movie review: Anurag Kashyap's Bandar follows fading star Samar Mehra (Bobby Deol) as a rape accusation pushes him into police custody and jail. The film dwells on systemic humiliation and prison brutality, even as its moral perspective narrows.

Putin's big Su-57 offer as India searches for its 5th-gen fighter jetVladimir Putin said Russia is ready to jointly develop and produce the Su-57 stealth fighter jet with India. The offer comes as the IAF seeks a fifth-generation fighter, while the AMCA remains years away from entering service.

Mohali man stabs ex-girlfriend to death in packed office, murder caught on CCTVThe victim was stabbed by her former partner and colleague, Harjinder Singh Mann. The fatal attack was captured on CCTV cameras installed at their workplace.

Were drumbeats for Rabi Lamichhane in India meant for PM Balen's ears in Nepal?Handcrafted ByOur Editors The grand welcome accorded to Rabi Lamichhane, Chairman of Nepal's ruling Rashtriya Swatantra Party, in India has made everyone sit up and take note. The question being asked is it a signal for PM Balendra 'Balen' Shah, whose remarks have triggered controversy in both Nepal and India?

Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai review: David Dhawan's formula finally expiresRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026David Dhawan returns to the formula that made him Bollywood's king of comedy - mistaken identities, chaotic romances and jokes that exist in their own universe. The problem is that the audience no longer does. What once felt outrageously entertaining now feels like a relic trying desperately to pass off as nostalgia.

What will happen to E20 vehicles when ethanol blending goes beyond 20%?As India prepares for the next phase of ethanol blending, questions are emerging over the future of millions of E20-compatible vehicles and whether they can adapt to higher ethanol concentrations. Let's find out.

Mamata Banerjee's Tollywood brigade missing amid TMC's worst crisisIt's all quiet on the Tollywood front. The Trinamool Congress's usually vocal, star-studded line-up of celebrity MPs and MLAs, who owe their seats to Mamata Banerjee's patronage, have either gone deafeningly silent or distanced themselves from the party as the TMC faces its worst existential crisis.

Bihar passengers haul giant metal trunk through train window, Railways reactsThe viral video, shared by an X account, shows an oversized metal trunk partially protruding from the windows of a moving train coach in Bihar. Two passengers appear to hold the trunk from inside the compartment while much of it hangs outside.

Skyfall: Did Suryakumar Yadav deserve to be sacked without a final act?Three months after lifting the T20 World Cup in Ahmedabad, Suryakumar Yadav has been sacked as India's captain. A late bloomer's extraordinary rise is over. But did he deserve better than this?

Uber and others go slow on AI, will billions spent by OpenAI, Anthropic go waste?Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Amazon and Microsoft are investing billions of dollars in AI. But of late, people have started asking the question: Is it a bubble? The trigger behind the question is the rising cost of AI that is forcing even top companies in the world to go easy on it.

India's 'Blue Mountains' in trouble. What's threatening the Nilgiris?On World Environment Day, let's have a look at the Nilgiris, famous for their mist-covered hills and diversity, now facing growing pressure from climate change and human activity.

Is Rishabh Pant's over-aggressive Test approach a problem? Coach Gambhir reactsIndia head coach Gautam Gambhir backed Rishabh Pant's aggressive style ahead of the Afghanistan Test but stressed that international cricket requires players to respect match situations. Pant remains under scrutiny following a series of questionable dismissals and his recent removal as India's Test vice-captain.

What Chief Minister DK Shivakumar said as minister, sulking over portfolio, quitKarnataka minister Ramalinga Reddy resigned from the cabinet just two days after taking oath, claiming he had twice been promised the Bengaluru Development portfolio before being assigned the Major and Medium Irrigation department instead.

Gujarat man repeatedly rapes teen daughter, gives her contraceptive pillsAs per a complaint filed by his wife, the man raped their daughter for three consecutive days in May and gave her contraceptive pills, while threatening to kill her if she told anyone about the assault.
Why Myntra, Amazon, and 30-minute deliveries haven't changed how I shop for clothesEach piece of clothing in my wardrobe has a story because I didn't just randomly buy it online.

Brown review: Karisma Kapoor's comeback is dark, messy and magneticRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026Brown series review: Karisma Kapoor returns in a psychological crime drama that looks beyond the murders. More than a mystery, Brown is a story of loss told through the eyes of a broken cop.

Sherpa went missing in Everest without food or oxygen. Then a miracle happenedA missing Nepali Sherpa guide was found alive on Mount Everest after six days. The rescue stunned his grieving family and officials after hope had nearly faded.

K Annamalai exits BJP, party chief Nitin Nabin accepts his resignationAnnamalai's resignation comes despite efforts within the BJP to persuade him to reconsider his decision to quit. As per party sources, the BJP leadership had remained hopeful that the former Tamil Nadu unit chief could be convinced to stay.

BJP gives up Andhra Rajya Sabha seat as K Annamalai exits partyUnder the revised formula, three of the four Rajya Sabha seats falling vacant in Andhra Pradesh will go to the Telugu Desam Party (TDP), while the remaining seat will be allotted to the Jana Sena Party (JSP).

sports.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026 big misses: Stars missing out on tournament due to injury As the build-up for the FIFA World Cup 2026 continues, these players will not be a part of the tournament due to injury. The likes of Rodrygo and Hugo Ekitike will be big misses for their sides as they chase the title.

Is Rishabh Pant's over-aggressive Test approach a problem? Coach Gambhir reacts India head coach Gautam Gambhir backed Rishabh Pant's aggressive style ahead of the Afghanistan Test but stressed that international cricket requires players to respect match situations. Pant remains under scrutiny following a series of questionable dismissals and his recent removal as India's Test vice-captain.

Sai Sudharsan or Devdutt Padikkal? Gambhir reveals India's No.3 for AFG Test Gautam Gambhir has indicated that Sai Sudharsan is ahead of Devdutt Padikkal for India's No. 3 spot in the Afghanistan Test. Gambhir backed Sudharsan, saying that he hasn't got a fair shot while batting at the spot.

5000 to 5 million: Least-known World Cup star meets influencer who made him famous Argentine influencer Valen Scarsini sought out Tim Payne after his online campaign turned the New Zealand defender into a viral World Cup figure. The episode shows how quickly digital culture can recast a fringe player as a tournament talking point.

Skyfall: Did Suryakumar Yadav deserve to be sacked without a final act? Three months after lifting the T20 World Cup in Ahmedabad, Suryakumar Yadav has been sacked as India's captain. A late bloomer's extraordinary rise is over. But did he deserve better than this?

Football fever grips Kerala, fans carry giant Brazil banner ahead of FIFA World Cup Kerala has welcomed the World Cup with giant banners, flares and rival colours on its streets. In Malappuram, the celebrations have turned anticipation into memory, as fans savour what they call a last dance.

Vinesh-WFI battle continues, wrestlers conduct at national trials under lens The Wrestling Federation of India has indirectly hinted that Vinesh Phogat's conduct at the national trials will come under the scanner after a chaotic tournament.

Group D Preview: Can hosts USA hold off Turkiye's golden generation? The United States head into a volatile Group D as co-hosts, with Turkiye, Paraguay and Australia all in contention. A wide-open race, led by a marquee USA-Turkiye clash, could leave qualification undecided until the final matchday.

After Carlsen, Praggnanandhaa beats Gukesh to go second at Norway Chess R Praggnanandhaa beat World Champion D Gukesh in Round 9 of Norway Chess 2026, moving into second place on 15 points, just half a point behind leader Wesley So. His winning streak has put him in strong title contention.

Once a failed transfer now the head coach, meet Iraola Liverpool's new boss Liverpool have appointed Andoni Iraola as head coach after sacking Arne Slot. The move revives a failed 2014 transfer and signals a reset towards the club's high-intensity identity.

Alexander Zverev eyes final berth; Matteo Arnaldi, Flavio Cobolli carry Italy's hopes Alexander Zverev is two wins away from a maiden Grand Slam title as he targets another French Open final, while rising Czech star Jakub Mensik stands in his path. Meanwhile, Italy is guaranteed a men's singles finalist.

Group C Preview: Neymar's last dance meets Morocco's red-hot challengeBrazil head into Group C as favourites, with Neymar back for another World Cup run. Morocco, Scotland and Haiti ensure the section looks far less straightforward than expected.

A fairytale in Paris: Maja Chwalinska turns struggle into stardom at French OpenFrench Open 2026: Poland's Maja Chwalinska arrived at Roland Garros as a little-known qualifier ranked outside the top 100, but has produced a fairytale run to reach her first Grand Slam final, defeating multiple top-50 players along the way.

Ollie Robinson runs riot as England seize control in Lord's Test vs New ZealandENG vs NZ, 1st Test: Ollie Robinson took four wickets on his comeback as England reduced New Zealand to 61 for six at Lord's after being bowled out for 140 on a rain-hit opening day in which 16 wickets fell.

Sri Lanka's fast-bowling dropout Rumesh Pathirage joins 90m javelin clubSri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage, a former fast bowler, set a new meet record in Rome Diamond League with a 92.62m throw on June 4. The 23-year-old became the fourth Asian after Neeraj Chopra, Arshad Nadeem and Cheng Chao-Tsun to breach the 90m mark in men's javelin.

Chwalinska creates history, becomes first qualifier to reach French Open finalFrench Open 2026: Qualifier Maja Chwalinska continued her dream run after beating Diana Shnaider 7-6(4), 6-4 in the women's singles semifinal on Thursday.

Shreyas Iyer replaces Suryakumar Yadav as India's T20I captainBCCI begins a new T20I era as Shreyas Iyer replaces World Cup-winning skipper Suryakumar Yadav. After successful IPL stints, Shreyas steps into a leadership role with the national team.

Pakistan skips Asian Senior Fencing Championships in New DelhiPakistan has declined India's invitation for the Asian Senior Fencing Championships in New Delhi. The decision comes even as India said Pakistani athletes were welcome at multilateral events.

Wicketkeeper-batter KS Bharat retires at 32 after falling down the pecking orderKS Bharat has retired from international cricket after a seven-Test career with India. The move follows his drop in the wicketkeeping pecking order and clears the path for overseas T20 opportunities.

RCB's Jacob Bethell faces the heat as Jamieson blows England away in Lord's TestEngland slid to 118 for 8 on the opening day at Lord's as New Zealand's pacers exposed a brittle batting line-up. Jacob Bethell's modest return intensified questions over his stint in the IPL in the lead-up to the Test series.

Mirra Andreeva heals old wounds, downs Marta Kostyuk to reach maiden major finalFrench Open 2026: Mirra Andreeva continued her dream run at Roland Garros, defeating Marta Kostyuk 6-1, 6-3 to reach her maiden Grand Slam final. The 19-year-old produced a composed and dominant display to end Kostyuk's 17-match clay-court winning streak.

Virat Kohli almost doesn't want the life he lives in India, says ex-RCB teammateFormer England all-rounder Liam Livingstone has revealed that Virat Kohli actively seeks to maintain a low profile in India, shedding light on the sharp contrast between the superstar's ultra-competitive on-field persona and his quiet family life.

Will Rashid Khan return for Tests? Afghanistan captain stays tight-lippedIND vs AFG: Hashmatullah Shahidi declined to clarify whether Rashid Khan will play Test cricket again before Afghanistan's match against India. His absence keeps the leg-spinner's red-ball future uncertain as Afghanistan seek more regular Tests.

Patidar is a strong contender for India's T20I captaincy: Ex-India selectorSaba Karim has put Rajat Patidar in the mix for India's T20I captaincy after his back-to-back IPL titles with RCB. The debate has widened ahead of the Ireland series, with selectors also considering Shreyas Iyer, Sanju Samson, Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma.

Semi-Finals Highlights: Chwalinska beats Shnaider, sets up final vs AndreevaFrench Open 2026 semifinals Live Updates: Maja Chwalinska has beaten Diana Shnaider in straight sets and has advanced to the final of Roland Garros. Earlier in the day, Mirra Andreeva beat Marta Kostyuk in straight sets to reach her maiden Grand Slam final.

Rohit Sharma doubtful for Afghanistan ODIs after Kohli setbackIndia are awaiting Rohit Sharma's fitness clearance for the Afghanistan ODI series after Virat Kohli was ruled out. The uncertainty has left selectors weighing replacement plans, with Yashasvi Jaiswal in contention and Hardik Pandya also under watch.

Did Dawood Ibrahim plot IPL takeover? Lalit Modi's bombshell claimsFormer IPL chief Lalit Modi has claimed that a plot by Dawood Ibrahim to control a tournament franchise and threats over disrupted illegal betting networks ultimately forced his complete exile from cricket administration.

Gaikwad likely to replace Kohli for Afghanistan ODI seriesVirat Kohli has been ruled out of the Afghanistan ODI series with a hamstring injury from the IPL 2026 final. Ruturaj Gaikwad is the leading candidate to replace him as selectors reshuffle India's squad.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Rose Bowl, SouthamptonThe Rose Bowl, Hampshire's current home ground, opened its doors in 2001 and represented a major step forward for the county's cricket infrastructure. Designed by renowned architects Michael Hopkins & Partners, the venue is known for its bowl-shaped layout and striking three-tier pavilion, which has become a defining feature of the stadium.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Old Trafford, ManchesterWomen's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Old Trafford in Manchester is one of England's most historic cricket grounds. Home to Lancashire CCC, it has hosted Test matches since 1884 and is known for its electric atmosphere. Old Trafford can accommodate up to 26,000 spectators.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
